name: Anthropic
description: Anthropic's Claude models are designed for safety and helpfulness. Claude excels at coding, analysis, and long-context reasoning. Available via claude.ai subscriptions and the Anthropic API.

pricing.notes: Free tier; rate-limited. Exact daily limits not publicly documented.

usage_limits.notes: Daily rate limits apply; exact count not publicly stated. Throttled during peak.

pricing.notes: No annual discount as of last check.

usage_limits.notes: ~5x more usage than Free tier per Anthropic docs. Exact limit not published as a hard number; varies by model and context length.

pricing.notes: Max plan: ~5x usage vs Pro. A $200/mo tier (20x Pro) was also advertised.

usage_limits.notes: ~5x Pro usage at $100/mo tier; ~20x Pro at $200/mo tier. No hard token cap published.

pricing.notes: Minimum 5 seats. Annual billing saves ~$5/seat/mo.

usage_limits.notes: Usage pooled across team. Exact limit not published.

benchmarks.name: HumanEval
benchmarks.notes: Approximate; verify against Anthropic model card

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench-verified
benchmarks.notes: Approximate; verify against Anthropic's published evals

provenance.notes: Prices and limits sourced from model knowledge; verify against live pricing page before publishing.

name: OpenAI
description: OpenAI's ChatGPT offers access to GPT-4o, o3, and o4-mini models for coding, reasoning, and general tasks. Codex CLI provides terminal-native AI coding. The Pro plan unlocks the most powerful models with expanded limits.

models.notes: Limited daily messages on GPT-4o; falls back to GPT-3.5 when limit hit

usage_limits.notes: Unofficial reports suggest ~10–30 GPT-4o messages per day. Not officially stated.

models.notes: Limited messages per day on o3

usage_limits.notes: GPT-4o is generous; o3 has a smaller daily allotment. Exact limits dynamic.

ide_integrations: VS Code (Copilot mode requires separate plan)

pricing.notes: Includes o1 Pro mode and extended o3 access.

usage_limits.notes: Marketed as 'unlimited' for most models; fair-use policy applies.

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench-verified
benchmarks.notes: gpt-4o single-attempt pass rate; verify against OpenAI evals

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench-verified
benchmarks.notes: o3 high-compute setting; verify against OpenAI published evals

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench-verified
benchmarks.notes: Approximate; verify against OpenAI evals page

provenance.notes: Prices from model knowledge; verify at openai.com/chatgpt/pricing before publishing.

name: GitHub Copilot
description: GitHub Copilot is an AI pair programmer deeply integrated into VS Code, JetBrains, and other IDEs. Provides inline code completions, chat, PR reviews, and CLI assistance. Powered by OpenAI models with GitHub-specific fine-tuning.

pricing.notes: Free for all GitHub users as of late 2024.

usage_limits.notes: 50 premium model chat messages per month on free tier.

pricing.notes: $10/mo or $100/yr. Annual saves ~$20/yr.

usage_limits.notes: Unlimited completions; chat messages have a higher allowance than free tier.

pricing.notes: Per seat, billed monthly. Minimum 1 seat.

features.notes: Admin policy controls; IP indemnification; no training on code.

pricing.notes: Requires GitHub Enterprise Cloud. Per seat/month.

features.notes: Codebase-aware chat (indexes your org's repos); PR summaries; fine-tuned models.

provenance.notes: Copilot uses multiple underlying models (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini) depending on task and user selection.
Pricing from model knowledge; GitHub frequently updates plans — verify at github.com/features/copilot#pricing.

name: Cursor
description: Cursor is an AI-native IDE (VS Code fork) with deep codebase context. Supports multi-file edits, AI terminal, agent mode, and lets you bring your own API key. Uses Claude, GPT-4o, and o-series models.

pricing.notes: Free tier. 2-week Pro trial for new users.

usage_limits.notes: After 50, must upgrade or use own API key.

features.notes: Can use own API key (BYOK) to bypass request limits.

pricing.notes: $20/mo or $192/yr ($16/mo). Annual saves $48/yr.

usage_limits.notes: Unlimited tab completions.
500 fast requests; additional slow requests available after. Resets monthly.

pricing.notes: $40/seat/month. Minimum seats may apply.

usage_limits.unit: fast premium model requests per seat

features.notes: Centralized billing; privacy mode; no training on code.

provenance.notes: Cursor proxies Anthropic Claude — benchmark scores are equivalent to claude-sonnet-4-6.
Cursor frequently updates pricing and fast-request limits — verify at cursor.com/pricing.

name: Google
description: Google's Gemini models power Google's AI assistant and API. Gemini Advanced provides the most capable models for coding and reasoning. Google AI Studio offers free API access for development. Gemini Code Assist provides IDE integration.

pricing.notes: Free access via gemini.google.com. Uses Gemini 2.0 Flash or 2.5 Flash.

usage_limits.notes: Daily rate limits apply; exact limit not published. Gemini Pro access limited on free tier.

pricing.notes: Part of Google One AI Premium. Includes 2TB storage and other Google benefits.

usage_limits.notes: No hard limit published. Subject to fair-use rate limits. Gemini 2.5 Pro has larger daily allowance than free.

features.notes: Includes Deep Research agent, extended Gems, Google Workspace integration.

pricing.notes: Free API access for development and experimentation. Rate-limited.

usage_limits.notes: Free-tier API: 15 RPM, 1M tokens/day for Gemini 2.5 Pro. Verify at ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/rate-limits.

features.notes: API-based access, not a consumer product. Useful for developers building on Gemini.

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench-verified
benchmarks.notes: Gemini 2.5 Pro; verify against Google DeepMind published evals

benchmarks.name: HumanEval
benchmarks.notes: Approximate; verify against official model card

provenance.notes: Google frequently rebrands and restructures plans; verify at one.google.com/intl/en/about/plans for Google One / Gemini Advanced pricing.

name: Kimi
description: Kimi is Moonshot AI's long-context conversational AI assistant with strong coding capabilities. Known for its 128K+ context window, code generation, and document analysis. Primarily serves Chinese and global markets.

pricing.notes: Free tier with daily message limits.

usage_limits.notes: Daily limit estimated; actual limit varies by model and may change.

pricing.notes: ¥59/mo. USD equivalent ~$8/mo at ~7.27 CNY/USD as of June 2026; actual USD price for international users may differ. Verify at kimi.ai/pricing.

usage_limits.notes: Higher daily limit than free; k1.5 usage may still be capped.

pricing.notes: ¥199/mo. USD equivalent ~$27/mo at ~7.27 CNY/USD as of June 2026. Verify at kimi.ai/pricing.

usage_limits.notes: Higher or unlimited usage across all Kimi models.

provenance.notes: Moonshot v1 series supports 8K, 32K, and 128K context variants.
Kimi k1.5 is a multimodal reasoning model. Benchmark scores not publicly disclosed at benchmark standard sites as of this writing.
Pricing primarily in CNY; USD equivalents estimated at ~7.27 CNY/USD as of June 2026. Verify at kimi.ai/pricing for current USD pricing for international users.

name: Qwen
description: Qwen is Alibaba Cloud's family of large language models, including the Qwen2.5-Coder series designed for code generation and completion. Accessed via the DashScope API. No subscription plans — API is pay-per-token with free trial credits.

pricing.notes: Free trial credits for new DashScope accounts. After credits are exhausted, pay-per-token billing begins.

usage_limits.notes: Free trial credit amount varies by region and promotion. Consumed by API calls.

features.notes: API-only access. No web UI for coding workflow. Integrate into your own tooling or IDE via OpenAI-compatible endpoints.

pricing.notes: Pay-per-token pricing. Costs vary by model; Qwen2.5-Coder models priced in USD per million tokens. Check dashscope.aliyuncs.com for current rates.

usage_limits.notes: No hard usage cap; billed per token consumed.

features.notes: Full API access with no monthly commitment. OpenAI-compatible endpoint available for easy integration.

provenance.notes: Qwen2.5-Coder-32B reported strong performance on HumanEval and SWE-bench but exact verified scores not confirmed here.
Qwen models accessed via DashScope API (dashscope.aliyuncs.com). Pricing is per-token; free trial credits available. No traditional subscription model.

name: Copilot for Xcode
description: GitHub Copilot for Xcode is the official Apple IDE extension bringing Copilot's AI code completion and chat directly into Xcode. Supports Swift, Objective-C, and Swift Playgrounds. Requires a GitHub Copilot subscription — same pricing as standard Copilot plans.

pricing.notes: Free for all GitHub users. Requires installing the Copilot for Xcode extension from GitHub.

usage_limits.notes: Same monthly completion allowance as GitHub Copilot Free.

features.notes: Xcode-only integration. Swift, Objective-C, and C/C++ code completion. Chat panel within Xcode.

pricing.notes: $10/mo or $100/yr. Shared GitHub Copilot Individual subscription — also unlocks Copilot in VS Code, JetBrains, etc.

features.notes: Subscription covers all Copilot integrations. Xcode support is one of several IDE options.

pricing.notes: $19/seat/month. Admin controls apply across all Copilot integrations including Xcode.

usage_limits.applies_to: completions and chat across all IDEs

features.notes: Admin policy controls; IP indemnification; no training on code. Xcode integration included alongside all other IDE support.

provenance.notes: Uses the same underlying Copilot models (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini) as the main Copilot product.
Copilot for Xcode is an official GitHub extension (OSS, MIT-licensed at github.com/github/CopilotForXcode). Pricing mirrors GitHub Copilot plans — no separate subscription.

name: OpenCode
description: OpenCode is a free, open-source AI coding agent for the terminal. MIT-licensed and BYOK (bring your own key) — connects to any OpenAI-compatible API including Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini, and local models. No subscription required.

pricing.notes: Free and open-source (MIT). You pay only for the LLM API keys you configure — OpenCode itself is always $0.

provenance.notes: MIT license confirmed at github.com/sst/opencode. No pricing model for the tool itself.

models.notes: Bring your own API key — Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible provider.

usage_limits.notes: No usage limits imposed by OpenCode — limits are set by your chosen LLM provider and API key quota.

features.notes: Terminal-native agentic coding agent. Supports file edits, shell commands, multi-step reasoning. Context window depends on your configured model. No built-in IDE integration — runs in any terminal.

provenance.notes: OpenCode is model-agnostic. Context length, quality, and benchmarks depend entirely on the model API you configure (e.g. Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o, Gemini, or a local Ollama model).

OpenCode is MIT-licensed open-source software. Free to use; cost depends on which LLM API keys you supply. No subscription or pricing page — cost is entirely pass-through to your chosen model provider.

name: Mimo
description: Mimo is an AI-powered coding education app that teaches programming through bite-sized, gamified lessons. NOT a professional AI coding assistant — it is a structured learning platform for beginners and career-changers learning to code. AI features assist with explanations and exercises rather than autonomous code generation.

pricing.notes: Free access to a limited set of courses and exercises.

usage_limits.notes: Free tier limits access to introductory content only.

features.notes: Educational platform only. Not suitable for professional coding work. Features are gamified lessons, progress tracking, and basic AI-powered explanations.

pricing.notes: $14.99/mo or ~$9.99/mo billed annually (~$119.99/yr). Verify at mimo.org/pro for current pricing.

usage_limits.notes: Unlimited access to the full course catalog, AI tutor explanations, and certificate programs.

features.notes: Full course library, AI explanations, offline access, certificates of completion. Educational platform — not for production code generation.

provenance.notes: Mimo AI Tutor is an educational assistant built into the platform. Underlying model not publicly disclosed.
Mimo is an educational platform, not an AI coding assistant for production developers. Plans listed here describe the learning subscription tiers. Headquarters in Vienna, Austria.

name: MiniMax
description: MiniMax is a Chinese AI research company offering large language models with exceptional long-context capabilities. Their MiniMax-Text-01 model supports up to 1 million token context. API-first platform; no subscription — pay-per-token via the MiniMax platform.

pricing.notes: Free trial credits for new accounts. After credits exhausted, pay-per-token billing.

usage_limits.notes: Trial credit amount varies; consumed by API requests.

features.notes: API-only access. Standout feature is 1M token context window — useful for large codebase analysis. No web IDE or chat UI for coding workflow.

pricing.notes: Pay-per-token pricing. Rates per million tokens vary by model. Check platform.minimaxi.com/document/pricing for current USD rates.

usage_limits.notes: No hard cap — billed per million tokens. 1M token context available on all paid requests.

features.notes: Full API access. 1M token context is the key differentiator — suited for analyzing very large codebases in a single request.

provenance.notes: MiniMax-Text-01 supports 1M token context. One of the longest context windows commercially available. Coding benchmarks not independently verified here.
MiniMax-M1 is MiniMax's reasoning-focused model. Supports 1M token context with reasoning capabilities.
MiniMax provides API access via platform.minimaxi.com. Pricing is per-token; no subscription tiers. USD pricing for international users may differ from CNY domestic pricing.
